task_completion.description: Weasis is open and displaying the CT image
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Weasis application window is visible with a grayscale medical image (CT) loaded in the main viewing area.
The Magnifier/Lens tool icon is highlighted, depressed, or selected in the Weasis toolbar, or visible in an active context menu.

task_completion.description: The magnifying glass overlay is visible on the image
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct magnifying glass overlay (typically a circular or rectangular zoomed region) is actively displayed on top of the CT image in the viewport.

task_completion.description: The magnifier is positioned over the brightest central structure
task_completion.visual_evidence: The magnifying glass overlay is positioned directly over the brightest white spot (simulated calculus) located in the center of the CT image, showing it magnified.

task_completion.description: A screenshot is saved to the specified export path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command (e.g., scrot, import), a screenshot GUI dialog, or a file manager window shows the screenshot being saved to or existing at '~/DICOM/exports/lens_view.png' or '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/lens_view.png'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Weasis application
integrity.visual_evidence: The Weasis interface (menus, toolbars, DICOM text overlays) is clearly visible, confirming the image wasn't just opened in a basic image viewer or web browser.

integrity.description: The magnifier effect is a genuine UI overlay, not a drawn shape
integrity.visual_evidence: The area inside the magnifier overlay actually shows a zoomed-in version of the underlying pixels, confirming it's a functional tool and not just a white circle drawn with an image editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI steps by copying a pre-existing file
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the agent actively opening Weasis, using the tool, and capturing the screen, rather than just executing a 'cp' or 'mv' command in the terminal to place a file in the exports folder.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output screenshot must be saved to exactly '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/lens_view.png'. The loaded CT image is a 512x512 synthetic DICOM with a distinct bright central structure (simulated calculus) that must be the target of the magnifier tool.
